series_description:
Adverse Action Review Committee
Packets.
comments: HIPAA compliant

series_title:
Caseload Profiles For
TANF/AFDC Food Stamps
And Medical Programs
series_description: Budget Background Documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 10 years
comments:
Within TIERS. OBSOLETE SERIES.
Delete after paper records are
destroyed.

series_description:
OIG and CQC Single Audit Unit. Working
papers. Includes closeout letters, audit
reports, financial statements, and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_title:
Data Supporting Special
Reports And Studies
series_description:
Special reports and studies not described
elsewhere on the schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
Documents that reflect changes to HHS
Program Handbooks.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
Vital Record. May include statewide
distribution.

series_description:
Records related to subcommittees created
at the discretion of HHS advisory
committees. Includes meeting
information, supporting documentation,
and other work products created by the
subcommittee.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_title:
Advisory Committee
Membership Statements
and Certificates
series_description:
Records related to the membership of
advisory committees, to include signed
statements by members, conflict-of-
interest disclosures (including Drug
Utilization Review Board members), and
certificates of completion of required
trainings, including Open Meetings Act
training and Ethics training.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Applications for membership on HHS
advisory committees. Includes
applications for individuals appointed to
committees and for individuals not
appointed.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Acknowledgement of
Receipt of Privacy Notice
(HIPAA)
series_description:
Retained by direct care providers as part of
the medical record. Only applies where
HHSC is the direct provider of care.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This series includes audits and reviews
performed by or on behalf of the agency.
Also includes field audits and audits
performed on the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
The State Auditor's Office (SAO)
retains any copies of its audits
performed on Texas state agencies.
See AINs 11386 and 11295 for Audit
Plan records.

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received by HHS from the public
concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
Complaints received about contractor or
provider services should also be retained
by the contract manager as part of the
contract monitoring record (see AIN 7032).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of AIN
5024. Additional remarks for
Medicaid only: When applicable,
any statutes of limitations must be
met before file is closed. Medicaid
complaints received in Health Plan
Management complaints inbox and
entered into the HHS Enterprise
Administrative Reporting and
Tracking System (HEART). The
HEART tracks external customer
complaints, issues, and resolutions
for compliance with SB 1563.
Additional records include the MCO
DMO complaints contact list,
provider, member or plan contacts.
Activity related to the oversight of
Medicaid programs by Centers for
Medicare and Medicaid Services
(CMS). Health Plan Operation (HPO)
Support and Finance participate in
the readiness reviews.

series_description:
Incoming/outgoing and internal leadership,
supervisory, managerial, executive,
administrative, substantive, and other high-
level correspondence in any media,
including electronic communication,
pertaining to the formulation, planning,
implementation, interpretation,
modification, or redefinition of the
programs, services, or projects of an
agency and the administrative regulations,
policies, and procedures that govern them.
Includes internal memos re: development
and admin of agency policies, procedures,
programs, and other major transactions.
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
AIN 5004 should be used only for
correspondence that is not included
in or directly related to another
records series on the agency's
approved schedule. For example, a
memorandum that documents an
appropriations request must be
retained for the minimum retention
period prescribed by AIN 5568; a
letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by AIN 5195, etc. See
also AIN 5006.

series_description:
Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and
internal correspondence, in any media, re:
routine matters, general inquiries, and
requests for materials. Including
electronic communication (e-mail)
pertaining to or arising from the routine
operations of the policies, programs,
services, or projects of an agency, sent or
received pursuant to law or ordinance in
connection with the transaction of public
business by any executive branch agency.
comments: See also AIN 5003 and 5005.

series_title:
Directives: Routine
Issuances, General
Administrative & Office
Procedures
series_description:
Any document that officially initiates,
rescinds, or amends general office
procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Records which document significant
changes or new agency policies, programs,
and/or procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected officials,
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of an agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process
of planning new or redefined programs,
services, or projects of an agency that are
neither included in nor directly related to
other records series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules; open
meetings notices; or any other documents
required by law to be submitted to the
Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Surveys soliciting feedback from customers
or clients of an agency, and the statistical
data maintained rating an agency's
performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
See AIN 5019 for summary reports
compiled from customer surveys.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
Instructional materials and other records
developed by an agency associated with
training the entities or individuals it
regulates or serves, including but not
limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in external training
programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
See AIN 5482 for internal personnel
training materials. CAUTION: Does
not include hazardous material
training records. See also AIN 5532.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register
persons appearing before state agencies as
required by Chapter 2004, Government
Code, including quarterly reports filed with
the Texas Ethics Commission (TEC).
Includes registration of persons who
represent others before state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and
2056.002. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan. Also includes revisions on
past plans by each division concerning
their program.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans With Disabilities Act.

Records of temporary usefulness that are
not an integral part of a records series of
an agency, that are not regularly filed
within an agency’s recordkeeping system,
and that are required only for a limited
period of time for the completion of an
action by an official or employee of the
agency or in the preparation of an ongoing
records series. Transitory records are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to the documentation of
agency functions. Some examples of
transitory information, which can be in any
medium (voice mail, fax, email, hard copy,
etc.) are routine messages; telephone
message notifications; internal meeting
notices; routing slips; incoming letters or
memoranda of transmittal that add
nothing of substance to enclosures; and
similar routine information used for
communication, but not for the
documentation, of a specific agency
transaction.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should use caution in
assigning this records series item
number to records of an agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part of
a records series that documents the
fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions. The
disposal of transitory information
need not be documented through
records disposition logs (AIN 5211),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (AIN 5212).

series_title:
Open Meetings, Agendas
and Minutes of -
Abolished
series_description:
Official Agendas and minutes of abolished
state boards, committees, commissions,
and councils that conducted open
meetings as required by Chapter 551,
Government Code.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
This series applies to state boards,
committees, and councils that were
abolished without being absorbed
by another board, committee or
council. For records for abolished
committees that were absorbed by
a current committee, see AIN 5207.

series_title:
Closed Meetings, Agendas
and Minutes or
Audiovisual Recordings of
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual
recordings of closed meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: See caution comment at AIN 5207.

series_description:
Audiovisual recordings of open meetings
of state boards, commissions, committees,
and councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
NOTE: Minutes of state agencies
are permanent records, audio and
videotapes are not. State agencies
may NOT retain audio or videotapes
of meetings in lieu of written
minutes. CAUTION: It is an
exception to the AC+2 retention
that if an agency does not prepare
written minutes, it must retain
recordings of open meetings
permanently, following the same
retention requirements of AIN
5207. See also caution comment at
AIN 5207.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments: SEE caution comment at AIN 5207.

series_description:
Documents submitted at meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils including exhibit documentation
for agenda items, public comment forms,
etc. Includes documents sent in advance of
meetings for briefing purposes, some of
which may not be submitted at an actual
meeting.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
See caution comment at AIN 5207.
Send original to the Archives and
Information Services Division, TSL
and Archives Commission.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency
staff meetings.

Information or data collected and
compiled for the purpose of producing non-
fiscal reports., Includes non-routing or
special reports, studies, surveys, etc.
Includes Raw Data materials not produced
in final report or study.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
See AIN 5019 for reports produced
from raw data. CAUTION: Does not
include source documentation used
for information or data included in
or directly related to another
records series in this schedule. See
AIN 5753.

series_description:
Biennial narrative reports to the governor
and legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes, including annual
narrative reports if they are required by
statute. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating final
report. The archival requirement is met by
sending the required copies of the reports
to the Texas State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and Archives
Commission.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_description:
Annual, sub-annual, administrative, special
reports or studies on non-fiscal aspects of
an agency's programs, services, or projects
compiled by agency personnel, by advisory
committees, or by consultants under
contract with an agency that are not noted
elsewhere in this schedule. Includes
reports distributed either internally or to
other entities.
comments:
See AIN 5020 for raw data used to
produce reports. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Reports are archival when they deal
with significant aspects of the
agency's programs.

series_description:
Quarterly and annual reports on agency
performance measures submitted to the
executive and legislative budget offices.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
See AIN 5753 for documentation
used to produce reports on agency
performance measures.

series_description:
Reports compiled by agency personnel on
a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, and
similar activities.
comments:
CAUTION: If reports are used to
document performance measures,
see AIN 5753.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records which define the
agency's policies and operating
procedures, such as employee's guide,
office procedures, manuals, etc., which are
distributed internally for the use of
employees or externally to the public or
those individuals or entities regulated by
an agency that sets out the rules, policies,
and procedures that govern an agency's
programs, services, or projects. Includes
working files and related documentation
used in creating rules, policies, and
procedures. Includes comments received
from the public after posting proposed
rules in the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Vital Record. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final rules, policies, and procedures,
are not subject to archival
requirement and may be disposed
of at the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description:
Includes Office of Chief Counsel
Administrative files, transcripts and final
decisions of hearings conducted as part of
the regulatory process, and hearings on
proposed rules and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes, and require a
longer retention period.

series_title:
Sunset Review Report and
Related Documentation
series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self- study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Waivers of liability, including statements
signed by volunteers acknowledging non-
entitlement to benefits, agreeing to abide
by state agency policies, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If an accident occurs to
any person covered by a signed
waiver of liability, it must be
retained for the same period as
accident reports. See AINs 5760,
11370, and 11371.

series_description:
Consists of Removal Log, Transmittal Log,
and Destruction Log.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments: Retained to meet IRS Requirements.

series_description:
Documentation used to provide and
improve access to records. Includes
indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers,
guides, and other finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_description:
Series includes one copy of each state
publication, except a publication that is
subject to a different retention period in
this schedule. For example, a meeting
agenda (see AIN 5207) also meets the
definition, but it must be retained
permanently; AINs 5002, 5434, 5017, 5016,
and 7015, which also meet the definition,
are closely associated with the
appropriations process and must be
retained AC+6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code, 441.103-
441.105). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will retain
a copy of all state publications
submitted to it on a continuing
basis, subject to periodic evaluation
to determine if the publication
merits further retention. For
additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the Internet, consult
the administrative rules of the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission (13 TAC
§§3.1-3.16). Web Publications are
made available online to public for
at least six months. Then retained
to meet their records series'
retention period or the period for
publications, which ever is longer.

series_description:
Physical and digital development and
design files used to create State
Publications, including but not limited to
background material, copy (drafts), original
artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc.
Series includes mechanicals, electronic
production masters, job tickets. Series is
most any printed product for the agency;
excluding forms. This includes all work
performed both inside and outside the
agency.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
See AIN 5510 for final State
Publications created from
development files. ARCHIVES NOTE:
Successive and substantive drafts of
major publications may be archival.
Major is defined by both the
publication's authorship and its
impact on Texas and Texans.
Original artwork, including photo
prints and negatives that have
significant value as evidence of
agency programs as well as the
potential for re-use, may be
archival.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records ; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include
but are not limited to: user guides, system
or sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts, program
flow charts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, data element dictionaries,
file layouts, code books or tables, and
other records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements. Includes
Code 3 Software Instruction Manuals - 3
Volumes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Vital Record. CAUTION: Software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents
establishing data processing policies and
procedures in an agency in such areas as
access and security, systems development,
data retention and disposition, data
ownership, production control, system
back-up, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading or
processing of electronic records.
See AIN 11250.

series_description:
A record of web pages visited during an
internet session, including data files of
user-specific information created by the
webserver, that allows users to access
previously visited pages more quickly or to
generate a record of usage of a state-
owned computer.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
The disposal of internet history
records need not be documented in
records disposition logs (AIN 5211),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (AIN 5212).

series_title:
Employee Health
Screening Records -
Hazardous Material, etc.
series_description:
Employee Health Screening Records -
Hazardous Material, Bloodborne Exposure,
Human Bite, Tuberculosis Exposure And
Needle Stick Incidents.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years
comments: See also AINs 11374/11375.

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those agencies
with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding
the terms and conditions of employment
of an individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding
the terms and conditions of employment
of an individual and a contract with a
vendor for temporary staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Medical or physical examination reports or
certificates of employees for whom
periodic monitoring of health or fitness is
required.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include pre-
employment physical examinations.
See AIN 5214. Does not include
medical or physical examinations
for employees exposed to
hazardous materials. See AINs
11374/11375.

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Does not include hazardous
material training records (see AIN
5532). Some training records are
included in the Master Personnel
File (see AIN 5047).

series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the
expenses of the employee are defrayed or
reimbursed (see also AIN 11447).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Any documents detailing the procedural
duties and responsibilities of agency
positions on a position-by-position basis.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the planning,
development, implementation,
administration and evaluation of in- house
training programs, including but not
limited to training manuals, course
registration, class rosters, sign-in sheets,
syllabuses, course outlines, and similar
training aids used in in-house training
programs. Includes materials and other
records associated with in-house training
of agency personnel on personnel policies
and procedures and other policies and
procedures that govern an agency's
programs, services, or projects.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
See AIN 5023 for external training
records. See AINs 5047/5052 for
individual employee training
records. CAUTION: Does not
include hazardous material or
HIPAA training records. See AIN
5532.

series_description:
FMLA Documentation includes all forms
documenting FMLA requests, FMLA status,
or use of FMLA benefits.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
FMLA documentation is maintained
by the employee's supervisor and is
not part of the Master Personnel
File. Part of 3.4.007 on State
Records Retention Schedule.

series_description:
This series documents state and federal
grant projects participated in or
administered by HHSC. It includes grant
authorization records, which provide
evidence of the award of grants to or by
HHSC; grant/project financial or
performance reports, which are periodic
reports of financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or
made by HHSC.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments: Vital Record.

series_description:
This series documents state, federal, and
other sponsored grant projects
participated in or administered by HHSC. It
includes grant authorization records, which
provide evidence of the award of grants to
or by HHSC; grant/project financial or
performance reports, which are periodic
reports of financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or
made by HHSC.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Vital Record. CAUTION: Retention
requirements may vary depending
on the specific funding agency.
Records in this series may be
subject to Government Code
441.1855. Agencies must ensure
that records are retained for the
appropriate retention period.

series_title:
Real Estate Deeds and
Corresponding Documents
series_description:
Facility Planning and Leasing. Real Estate
Management retains the agency originals.
In some cases, the General Land Office
may retain agency originals.
retention_statement: Life of Asset

series_title:
Contract Monitoring and
Compliance (9/1/2015 and
later)
series_description:
Records and information resulting from
the monitoring of contract requirements
and deliverables. Includes creation of
corrective actions and response as a result
of State Action Requests (SARs); meetings
with quality or performance groups; and
reports used to monitor contract
requirements and deliverables. Includes
financial information related to the
oversight of billings and expenditures in

series_title:
Plans of Correction and
Evidence Books
series_description:
Plans of correction and evidence books are
utilized by facilities to prove that cited
deficiencies from regulatory services, we
addressed and that other individuals who
may be affected from the deficient practice
were identified and addressed to ensure
the deficient practice is unlikely to occur.
Evidence can be a wide variety of items,
depending on the citation. This can include
IDT meetings, committee meeting minutes,
fire drill forms, environmental checks,
training rosters, etc. The POC books and
evidence are needed until the citation can
be cleared by regulatory services. Because
there is no ACC (automatic cancelation
clause) as in years past, deficiencies may
remain open for longer periods of time (as
much as a year from annual to annual).
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 3 years

series_title:
LOS Cards, Home Shift and
Hygiene Logs
series_description:
LOS Cards are records showing the level of
supervision (how closely a client needs to
be supervised by staff for safety) each
individual. Home Shift Log are kept every
day on each home. They are a general
record of events for the day for both
clients and staff. They contain PHI for
multiple residents. They may be used to
review who was present during a shift or
what events and incidents occurred that
day. One of the common uses is for

series_title:
Contract Monitoring and
Compliance (8/31/2015
and prior)
series_description:
Records and information resulting from
the monitoring of contract requirements
and deliverables. Includes creation of
corrective actions and response as a result
of State Action Requests (SARs); meetings
with quality or performance groups; and
reports used to monitor contract
requirements and deliverables. Includes
financial information related to the
oversight of billings and expenditures in
support of the contract; and, data and
reports needed to monitor service
coordination, call center services, claims
processes, encounters, timely submission,
marketing materials, and member
inquiries, complaints and resolution.,
Includes Contract Monitoring Records
Found in TeamMate. Includes any
remediation required to correct non-
compliance. Desk reviews and onsite
reviews. May include the following:
contract with vendors, each program's
monitoring correspondence, monitoring
reports, and clearing of findings. Applies to
contracts that were executed, extended, or
renewed on or before August 31, 2015
(See 7039/7040).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
Vital Record. SCOR may contain
additional contract monitoring
records. For Medicaid/CHIP
contract monitoring for 8/31/2015
and prior, see AIN 7031. See also
AIN 7032.

series_description:
Includes action plans, forecasting, and
related records used to ensure timely
contract executions by PCS. Includes
Procurement Forecasting and Action Plans
(PFAPs) created by HHS agency/divisions
using Form PCS 357 and updates to the
plans as required per PCS Policy 357.
Includes documents and information from
HHS agency/divisions used in preparing
PFAPs and similar reports for PCS.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
PCS maintains final PFAPs
submitted annually by November
1st from HHS agency/divisions and
quarterly updates in PCS Contract
Management Support SharePoint
site. HHS Program and Support
areas completing the Procurement
and Forecasting Action Plan are
responsible for maintaining any
working papers used to develop the
plan and area approvals of the
annual submission and any
subsequent revisions or updates to
the plan. In addition, IT obtains and
documents purchasing authority on
the PFAP report. See AINs 7070,
7071, 7072, 11399 for retention of
official procurement files.
